reasoning: Elon Musk left his official role as head of DOGE (a Special Government Employee position) on May 29, 2025, after reaching his 130-day legal limit and severely clashing with President Trump over policy issues. The fallout resulted in a public feud, with Trump stating he 'asked him to leave' as Musk was 'wearing thin'. By late September 2025, betting markets (Kalshi and Polymarket) assessed the probability of Musk rejoining the administration by the end of 2025 at around 7-8%. Since we are now in early November with no signs of an official reconciliation or an effort to work around the legal 130-day SGE exclusion period (which extends into 2026), time decay forces the probability down further. Any non-SGE position would likely require Musk to divest from his sprawling business empire (Tesla, SpaceX, xAI, etc.), which is fundamentally implausible. With scarcely seven weeks remaining in 2025, the likelihood of a formal, publicly announced appointment before December 31 is vanishingly small. I am placing the probability at the minimum structural bound for extremely unlikely estimates: 5%.
